subject: [PATCH v2 0/3] mm: bypass swap readahead for zswap

reviews.author: David Hildenbrand
reviews.summary: Raised questions about the performance benefits and potential issues with the new approach. Suggested testing and validation before merging.

reviews.author: Alexandre Ghiti
reviews.summary: Addressed David's concerns by providing additional testing results and explaining the benefits of the new approach.

2026-07-22.patch_summary: This patch series aims to optimize swap readahead for zswap by splitting the window walk into two passes and reordering it around the distinction between synchronous and asynchronous device reads. The first pass overlaps the synchronous work with disk reads, while the second pass is skipped when the target needs no I/O. This approach helps mixed workloads (zswap + disk) and keeps synchronous-target page-fault latency low.
